title: BanglaForge: LLM Collaboration with Self-Refinement for Bangla Code Generation
problem: Most existing code generation systems are designed for English inputs, leaving low-resource languages underserved. Models struggle with informal structures, domain-specific terms, and semantic nuances in languages like Bangla.
solution: BanglaForge - a framework for generating executable code from Bangla task descriptions.

input_representation.description: Triple of Bangla description, English translation, and unit test assertions
input_representation.purpose: Leverages model's stronger English understanding while retaining Bangla context

key_components.name: Retrieval-augmented prompting
key_components.method: TF-IDF to select relevant Bangla-Python pairs
key_components.purpose: Improves in-context learning despite limited labeled data

key_components.name: LLM-based translation component
key_components.method: Translates Bangla instructions into English with glossary assistance

key_components.name: Iterative self-refinement protocol
key_components.method: Leverages execution feedback to detect and correct errors across refinement cycles
key_components.purpose: Improves code quality through multiple refinement cycles

key_components.name: Dual-model architecture
key_components.purpose: Generator focuses on functional correctness, reviewer enhances robustness, style, and coverage of edge cases

workflow.description: Coder LLM generates Python code and test cases
Reviewer LLM validates, refines, and re-prompts until all tests pass

related_work.evolution: From word embeddings to specialized LLMs for Bangla
related_work.gap: Existing work largely focuses on pretraining and benchmarking without complete generation pipelines

dataset.source: Bangla Code Generation shared task (Task 2) at BLP-2025
dataset.objective: Translate Bangla natural language programming prompts into Python functions that satisfy hidden unit tests
